svg.text: TURKISH
PHONOLOGY & VOWEL HARMONY
TURKISH VOWEL HARMONY
Vowels must agree in backness and rounding within a word:
Back vowels: a ı o u • Front vowels: e i ö ü
Suffix vowels change to match root vowel:
göz-ü (eye-his/her) • el-i (hand-his/her) — ü matches ö; i matches e
köy-den (from village) • şehir-den (from city) — den vs. den
TURKISH CONSONANTS
Stops: p b t d k g (+ ç and ğ)
Fricatives: f v s z ş /ʃ/ h
Final devoicing: b→p d→t g→k at word end
kitab "book" (Arabic) → Turkish: kitap (final devoicing)
TURKISH VOWELS (8)
a /a/ e /e/ ı /ɨ/ i /i/ o /o/ ö /ø/ u /u/ ü /y/
/ɨ/ (dotless i): unique — absent from most European languages
KURDISH PHONOLOGY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
KURMANJI CONSONANTS
Additional consonants not in Turkish:
/q/ uvular stop • /x/ velar fricative • /w/ bilabial approximant
/ʕ/ voiced pharyngeal (from Iranian heritage)
These are Iranian-origin sounds not in Turkic
Kurdish VOWELS
Kurmanji: a e ê i î o u û (long vowels with circumflex)
NO vowel harmony (unlike Turkish) — Iranian language feature
Sorani: similar vowel inventory, Arabic-script based
PHONOLOGICAL CONTRAST
Turkish: 8 vowels with vowel harmony system
Kurdish: vowels no harmony (different family entirely)
Both: /tʃ/ "ch" sound • both: final devoicing tendency
Kurdish: uvular /q/ and /x/ (Turkic lacks native /q/)
Contact: Turkish borrowed some Kurdish sounds; Kurdish Turkish words
PLATE 51b ALT · TURKISH / KURDISH · PHONOLOGY
